title: GitHub Review by gndrmnn
summary: The reviewer wants the backwards-compatibility comments removed from three locations (the two CRD YAMLs and `keystone_types.go:84`), since the project has no stable release and breaking changes are acceptable.

code_comments.body: This project has never had a stable release yet. The definitions are fine to break. Nobody uses this productively yet. Remove the comment about backwards compatibility.
code_comments.author: gndrmnn
code_comments.resolution: Removed the 'preserved for backwards compatibility with envtest fixtures... legacy bypass-only path' sentences from the trustFlush description block in operators/keystone/config/crd/bases/keystone.openstack.c5c3.io_keystones.yaml; the description now ends at 'set suspend: true.'

code_comments.body: This project has never had a stable release yet. The definitions are fine to break. Nobody uses this productively yet. Remove the comment about backwards compatibility.
code_comments.author: gndrmnn
code_comments.resolution: Removed the same backwards-compatibility sentences from the trustFlush description block in operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/crds/keystone.openstack.c5c3.io_keystones.yaml so the helm-shipped CRD matches the config/crd/bases copy.

code_comments.body: This project has never had a stable release yet. The definitions are fine to break. Nobody uses this productively yet. Remove the comment about backwards compatibility.
code_comments.author: gndrmnn
code_comments.resolution: Removed the trailing backwards-compatibility sentences from the TrustFlush godoc on KeystoneSpec.TrustFlush in operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_types.go; the comment now ends at 'set suspend: true.', which is the source of the regenerated CRD YAMLs.

resolution_summary: Removed the trailing sentences about preserving the pointer shape 'for backwards compatibility with envtest fixtures' from the TrustFlush field's godoc in operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_types.go. The two CRD YAMLs (config/crd/bases and helm/keystone-operator/crds) are generated from that godoc, so the same paragraph was regenerated/edited there as well, addressing all three review comments at once. No additional unrelated files were touched.
